re_line: Kim tests tactical nukes targeting ROK power grids · KOSPI circuit breaker triggered · ex-first lady sentenced · ROK-Japan defense ministers meet
morning_memo: Kim Jong Un oversaw tests of weapons explicitly designed to strike ROK power stations with tactical nuclear warheads, per KCNA, on the 76th anniversary of the Korean War — one day after commissioning the Choe Hyon destroyer and convening a Workers' Party plenum pledging to 'overtake the world' militarily.

The KOSPI plunged nearly 6 percent and triggered a circuit breaker, its steepest single-day drop in recent months, as profit-taking followed a sustained AI-led rally — marking the second consecutive week of sharp Seoul market volatility.

Former first lady Kim Keon Hee was sentenced to 7 years in prison by a Seoul court for accepting gifts in exchange for job appointments, delivering the highest-profile judicial verdict of the post-Yoon era and arriving the same day a new Gallup Korea poll showed President Lee Jae-myung's approval at its lowest level since taking office.

on_this_day.event: North Korea invaded South Korea, beginning the Korean War — the 76th anniversary falls on June 25, 2026.
on_this_day.relevance: Kim Jong Un's weapons tests on June 25-26, 2026 — including tactical nuclear strike drills targeting ROK power stations — were explicitly timed to the Korean War anniversary, continuing a DPRK pattern of using June 25 for high-profile military demonstrations.

key_stat.label: Prison sentence handed to former first lady Kim Keon Hee
key_stat.context: A Seoul court sentenced Kim Keon Hee to 7 years in prison for accepting gifts in exchange for job appointments, the most consequential judicial verdict against a former presidential family member in the post-Yoon era.

imagery_report.label: Korean Peninsula Update — AEI
imagery_report.headline: AEI publishes June 23 Korean Peninsula Update covering DPRK facility and military activity
imagery_report.body: AEI's June 23, 2026 Korean Peninsula Update covers recent DPRK military and facility developments. The update follows AEI's June 2026 special report on DPRK constitutional amendments noting the nuclear program's posture is now formally entrenched in state law. No new facility-specific imagery findings are described in the available article summary beyond those already tracked.

bp_locations.name: Yongbyon Nuclear Complex
bp_locations.note: Apr 2026: Enrichment building expansion complete, new centrifuge hall operational. 5 MWe reactor and IRT-2000 both running. Thermal signatures consistent with increased plutonium/HEU production. AEI's June 2026 special report on DPRK constitutional amendments notes the nuclear program's posture is now formally entrenched in state law.

bp_locations.name: Sinpo South Shipyard
bp_locations.note: Feb 2025: Hero Kim Kun Ok SLBM submarine exited drydock after 2+ years of fitting-out but still not operational as of May 2025. New submarine construction begun in separate bay.

bp_locations.name: THAAD Site — Seongju County
bp_locations.note: Jun 21, 2026: All six THAAD launchers returned to the permanent Seongju site from Osan Air Base, restoring the battery's full deployment footprint. Battery operational status remains subject to ongoing assessment after the partial Middle East redeployment.

bp_locations.name: Sohae Satellite Launch Station
bp_locations.note: May 2026: Rocket engine test conducted last week, possibly for military reconnaissance satellite program revival. New viewing area building nearing completion. Previous reports showed 2,500 kN engine test stand firing and facility expansion.

bp_locations.name: Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site
bp_locations.note: Nov 2025: ROK DIA assessed Tunnel No. 3 is test-ready and a nuclear test can be conducted at any time upon political decision. Site maintained at high readiness since mid-2024.

bp_locations.name: Tumangang–Khasan (NK-Russia border)
bp_locations.note: Jun 19, 2026: Satellite imagery shows new Tumen River vehicle bridge nearing completion with finishing touches underway but customs infrastructure not yet complete on either side; scheduled opening June 19, 2026. The Economist (Jun 21) reports the bridge is nearly complete. Previous reporting: new road bridge construction accelerated, completion target moved to Summer 2026. Passenger rail service resumed mid-2025. Ore and tank car traffic at highest levels since project began.

bp_locations.name: Sinuiju–Dandong (NK-China border)
bp_locations.note: Mar 12, 2026: Cross-border passenger train service resumed between Sinuiju and Dandong after COVID-era suspension. New Yalu River Bridge may open in 2026.

bp_locations.name: Rason SEZ
bp_locations.note: Nov 2025: Chinese businesspeople re-entry to Rason SEZ expanded. Coal exports through Rason at highest levels in years throughout 2025. Arms export berth also active.

bp_locations.name: Yellow Sea NLL
bp_locations.note: Sep 26, 2025: 140m DPRK merchant vessel Toksong crossed NLL near Baengnyeong Island, advanced 5km south. ROK Navy fired ~60 warning shots; vessel retreated after 1 hour. Ship switched AIS nationality to China and flew Chinese flag. First NLL intrusion in three years.

bp_locations.name: Yellow Sea PMZ
bp_locations.note: Atlantic Amsterdam (converted oil rig, Chinese aquaculture platform) relocated out of PMZ Jan 27-28, 2026 to Weihai shipyard following Lee Jae-myung Beijing state visit. Two giant aquaculture cages (Shen Lan 1, Shen Lan 2) and 13 buoys remain in the PMZ.

bp_locations.name: Vostochny/Dunai (Russian Far East)
bp_locations.note: Jan 2026: Arms shipments slowed due to ice — one Russian vessel (likely Angara or Lady R) visited Rason, docked Jan 14 at arms export berth. Cargo routed to Vostochny. Since Sep 2023: 64 voyages by 4 vessels delivered ~15,800 containers from Rajin to Vostochny/Dunai.

rok_government.action: Lee stresses technology as national security pillar, calls for fostering innovative firms
rok_government.detail: President Lee Jae-myung stated that technology is key to national security and called for government support to cultivate innovative companies. The remarks came the same day Lee announced three more South Korean vessels are expected to exit the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend.

rok_government.action: Lee denies NSA met Russian official in Kazakhstan
rok_government.detail: Cheong Wa Dae denied a news report that National Security Adviser held a meeting with a Russian official in Kazakhstan. The denial came amid continued ROK diplomatic sensitivity over any perception of covert Russia engagement.

rok_government.action: Lee vows pragmatic diplomacy to tackle global uncertainties
rok_government.detail: President Lee Jae-myung pledged at a forum that his government would pursue pragmatic diplomacy in response to shifting global alliances and economic security challenges. The remarks were delivered at a Yonhap News Agency-hosted forum on alliances and economic security.

rok_government.action: ROK FM discusses Hormuz transit and Mideast situation with Iranian counterpart by phone
rok_government.detail: Foreign Minister Cho Hyun spoke by phone with his Iranian counterpart to discuss South Korean vessel transit through the Strait of Hormuz and the broader Middle East situation. As of June 26, eight more South Korean-operated vessels had exited the Strait, with three more expected over the weekend.

rok_government.action: ROK defense minister announces deployment of 'K-Lucas' long-range suicide drones
rok_government.detail: South Korea's defense minister announced plans to field 'K-Lucas' long-range suicide drones to bolster the country's unmanned weapons system. The announcement comes as South Korea is simultaneously training 'drone warriors' in response to North Korea's weapons buildup.

rok_government.action: Unification minister calls for moving away from 'denuclearization first' policy toward North Korea
rok_government.detail: Unification Minister Chung Dong-young called for South Korea to move beyond a 'denuclearization first' framework in its North Korea policy, signaling an openness to engagement that does not condition all dialogue on prior denuclearization commitments. The statement represents a notable doctrinal shift from the previous administration's approach.

calendar_watch.headline: Japan Defense Minister Koizumi visits South Korea (Jun 27-28)
calendar_watch.detail: Japan's Defense Minister Koizumi arrives in Seoul for bilateral defense ministerial talks. Meeting addresses shared concerns over North Korea's accelerating weapons program and trilateral cooperation.

calendar_watch.headline: BOK Monetary Policy Board rate decision
calendar_watch.detail: Bank of Korea holds its next monetary policy board meeting. Current benchmark rate is 2.50% following the June 2026 cut; markets will watch for further easing signals amid KOSPI volatility.

calendar_watch.headline: Section 122 surcharge expiry — 150-day statutory limit
calendar_watch.detail: The US 10% global Section 122 tariff surcharge expires unless Congress acts to extend it. Post-expiry tariff authority for Korean goods reverts to the US-Korea Strategic Trade deal rate structure and ongoing Section 301 investigations.

calendar_watch.headline: Korean Liberation Day (광복절)
calendar_watch.detail: National holiday; ROK President Lee Jae-myung expected to deliver Liberation Day address with policy signals on inter-Korean relations and alliance posture.

calendar_watch.headline: Ulchi Freedom Shield (UFS) — annual US-ROK combined exercise (mid-August)
calendar_watch.detail: Annual US-ROK combined military exercise, typically 11 days in mid-August. Expect DPRK rhetorical escalation; this year's exercise follows the Choe Hyon commissioning and Kim's nuclear arsenal buildup directive.

top_stories.headline: North Korea tests ability to strike ROK power stations with tactical nukes, per KCNA
top_stories.body: KCNA reported that Kim Jong Un oversaw weapons tests specifically designed to demonstrate the DPRK's ability to strike South Korean power stations using tactical nuclear warheads, conducted on the 76th anniversary of the Korean War. The tests followed Kim's June 25 Workers' Party plenum directive to 'exercise its position as a nuclear state' and his June 25 commissioning of the Choe Hyon destroyer. The CSIS provocations database records a MIRV capability test announced exactly two years earlier on June 26, 2024.
top_stories.so_what: Directly affects the agenda for the June 27-28 ROK-Japan defense ministerial talks in Seoul.
top_stories.pattern_note: On June 26, 2024, North Korea announced its first MIRV capability test; the June 26, 2026 tactical nuclear power-grid strike test marks a two-year pattern of Korean War anniversary provocations escalating in declared capability.

top_stories.headline: Seoul court sentences former first lady Kim Keon Hee to 7 years for jobs-for-gifts bribery
top_stories.body: A Seoul court sentenced former first lady Kim Keon Hee to 7 years in prison for accepting gifts in exchange for job appointments — the most consequential judicial verdict against a former presidential family member in the post-Yoon era. The verdict arrives as President Lee Jae-myung's approval hits its lowest point since taking office, per a new Gallup Korea poll released the same day.
top_stories.so_what: The verdict may sharpen PPP efforts to contest the political narrative against the former administration while Lee faces domestic approval pressure from a separate ballot-shortage scandal.

top_stories.headline: KOSPI circuit breaker triggered as Seoul shares nosedive nearly 6 percent on profit-taking after AI-led rally
top_stories.body: South Korea's bourse operator activated a circuit breaker on Friday as the KOSPI fell nearly 6 percent — its steepest single-session decline in recent months — driven by profit-taking following a sustained AI-led rally. The KRX also activated a sell-side sidecar earlier in the session. The KOSPI closed at 8,411.21, down 7.08 percent, with USD/KRW moving to 1,537.58.
top_stories.so_what: The sharp selloff increases pressure on the BOK ahead of its July 10 rate decision and may prompt government stabilization measures.

top_stories.headline: South Korea and Japan defense ministers to hold talks in Seoul, June 27-28
top_stories.body: Defense ministers of South Korea and Japan will meet in Seoul over the weekend of June 27-28, per Yonhap. Japan's Defense Minister Koizumi will travel to Seoul for the bilateral talks, which come one day after Kim Jong Un oversaw tactical nuclear weapons tests targeting ROK infrastructure. The meeting is the first high-level ROK-Japan defense contact since the Choe Hyon commissioning and Workers' Party plenum nuclear declarations.
top_stories.so_what: The talks will address DPRK weapons developments and trilateral coordination — directly on the agenda given the June 25-26 DPRK activity.

overnight_items.headline: NK Pro satellite imagery: new activity at DPRK military parade training base
overnight_items.body_text: NK Pro analysis of Planet Labs satellite imagery shows North Korea's main military parade training base has become active again in recent days, suggesting Pyongyang may hold another parade in 2026. The new activity follows large-scale parades in October 2025 and February 2026, each requiring months of formation training for over 10,000 troops. A parade would provide a high-visibility platform to display the Choe Hyon destroyer crew, new weapons systems tested June 25-26, and potentially new strategic assets referenced in the Workers' Party plenum.

overnight_items.headline: DPRK hackers deploy new macOS malware 'macOS.Gaslight' that manipulates AI security tools
overnight_items.body_text: North Korean-linked cybercriminals have deployed a newly identified malware family called 'macOS.Gaslight,' which combines traditional data-stealing functions with a novel capability designed to manipulate AI-powered security tools, according to U.S. cybersecurity firm SentinelLabs. The malware targets Apple computers and was identified in a report published Tuesday. The innovation of targeting AI-based defenses marks an escalation in DPRK cyber tradecraft beyond conventional endpoint evasion.

overnight_items.headline: Russian companies court North Korea with fuel, fertilizer, and construction bids
overnight_items.body_text: Russian companies have been actively soliciting North Korean contracts offering fuel, fertilizer, and construction services, per NK News reporting, signaling expanding commercial dimensions of the NK-Russia relationship beyond weapons transfers. The outreach follows Kim Jong Un's June 25 Workers' Party plenum pledge of 'full support and solidarity' to Russian forces in Ukraine. Commercial bidding by Russian firms for DPRK contracts represents a deepening economic integration layer alongside the military cooperation tracked in the CSIS NK-Russia timeline.

overnight_items.headline: ROK Unification Minister calls for abandoning 'denuclearization first' North Korea policy
overnight_items.body_text: Unification Minister Chung Dong-young called on Friday for South Korea to move away from a framework that conditions all engagement with North Korea on prior denuclearization steps. The call comes as former nuclear envoys at a Jeju forum simultaneously assessed that a potential Kim-Trump summit remains 'most important' to resolving the nuclear issue but that talks are 'not yet ripe.' The shift is particularly striking given KCNA's June 25-26 tactical nuclear weapons test reporting.

overnight_items.headline: Lee Jae-myung approval sinks to record low since taking office, per new Gallup Korea poll
overnight_items.body_text: President Lee Jae-myung's approval rating dropped to its lowest point since his inauguration, according to a Gallup Korea poll released Friday. The Chosunbiz-cited result shows approval at 51 percent — down from 57 percent in the June 9-11 poll — with the ballot shortage scandal cited as the primary driver. The dip arrives the same day former first lady Kim Keon Hee was sentenced, creating a politically complex news cycle for both the ruling Democratic Party and the PPP.

overnight_items.headline: Korean American congresswoman calls for ROK-US-Japan joint deterrence against North Korea, China, Russia
overnight_items.body_text: A Korean American congresswoman speaking at a Seoul forum called for strengthened South Korea-US-Japan trilateral deterrence specifically directed at the threat axis of North Korea, China, and Russia. The call came the day after Kim Jong Un's Workers' Party plenum pledged solidarity with Russia and announced naval expansion targeting ROK infrastructure. The statement aligns with the Camp David trilateral framework commitments and directly precedes the ROK-Japan defense ministers meeting on June 27-28.

also_today.headline: US nuclear expert urges patience on ROK-US nuclear submarine cooperation
also_today.body_text: A US nuclear expert at the Jeju forum cautioned that nuclear submarine cooperation between South Korea and the United States requires patience and caution, flagging technical, legal, and alliance management complexities. The remarks add expert texture to one of the most sensitive extended deterrence debates in the bilateral alliance.

also_today.headline: Ex-nuclear envoy: Kim-Trump summit 'most important' to resolving North Korea nuclear issue
also_today.body_text: A former South Korean nuclear envoy told a Jeju forum that a potential Kim-Trump summit would be the single most important mechanism for resolving the North Korean nuclear issue, even as a separate former Trump adviser assessed that conditions for talks are 'not yet ripe.' The diverging assessments reflect ongoing uncertainty over whether the second Trump administration will pursue direct engagement.

also_today.headline: South Korea to dispatch public-private delegation to Europe to expand space cooperation
also_today.body_text: South Korea plans to send a public-private delegation to Europe to deepen space cooperation, per MOTIE/government sources, as Seoul diversifies its space partnerships amid DPRK satellite launch program competition and US-China rivalry in the space domain.

also_today.headline: The Political Risks Facing Washington's New Ambassador to South Korea
also_today.body_text: The Diplomat examines the political complexities facing Michelle Steel as she takes up the US ambassador role in Seoul, where ethnic identity politics and domestic polarization intersect with alliance management. The piece highlights how ambassador-level diplomacy operates in a charged environment as the Lee administration faces approval headwinds.

investment_package.latest_update: No new WH-verified deal announced today; tracker stands at $99.1B of $350B (28%) as of last verified entry (Korea Zinc/Crucible Metals, Dec 2025).

known_deals.sector: Manufacturing — Georgia EV, Louisiana steel, robotics hub
Engines and maintenance for Boeing fleet
Power grid infrastructure — undersea cables, power equipment
Defense — 4 AEW&C aircraft for ROK Air Force
Critical Minerals — US rare earth separation/refining

known_deals.company: Samsung Heavy Industries / Vigor Marine Group

trade_policy.detail: Launched Mar 11 2026 against 16 trading partners including South Korea; could provide new legal authority for tariffs post-Section 122 expiry (Jul 24 2026). Active investigation ongoing.

trade_policy.item: BIS Semiconductor Export Controls — Samsung and SK Hynix China fabs
trade_policy.detail: Samsung and SK Hynix China fabs operating under annual BIS export licenses renewed Dec 30 2025 for 2026; maintenance and incremental upgrades only, no expansion. Shifted from indefinite VEU waivers to restrictive annual licenses Jan 2026.

trade_policy.item: IRA Battery Sourcing Rules (FEOC) — Korean EV battery makers
trade_policy.detail: Foreign Entity of Concern rules affect LG Energy Solution, SK On, and Samsung SDI sourcing from China. Battery component phase-in Dec 2024; critical minerals phase-in Dec 2025. Both now active.

trade_policy.item: CHIPS Act Guardrails — Samsung Taylor TX fabs
trade_policy.detail: Samsung received preliminary $6.4B CHIPS award (Apr 2024) for Taylor TX fabs; 10-year restriction on materially expanding semiconductor manufacturing in countries of concern. Samsung Xi'an NAND fab limited to legacy production.

tariff_tracker.headline_note: 10% Section 122 global surcharge (effective Feb 24 2026) applies to South Korean goods; expires Jul 24 2026 unless Congress acts; base US-Korea Strategic Trade deal rate of 15% (IEEPA/EO 14257) remains the agreed bilateral framework rate subject to Supreme Court ruling.

sector_rates.note: All country exemptions eliminated Mar 2025; no quota arrangement under US-Korea deal.
Reduced under US-Korea deal from 25%; retroactive to Nov 1 2025 per USTR Dec 3 2025 implementation notice.
Reduced under US-Korea deal; same USTR Dec 3 2025 implementation notice.
Effective Jan 15 2026; broader semiconductor tariff scope TBD; deal promises Korea terms no less favorable than comparable trading partners.
Rate cap agreed in US-Korea deal but specific Section 232 action not yet issued.
Tariffs removed per USTR Dec 3 2025 notice.

sector_rates.sector: Generic Pharma / Precursors / Rare Natural Resources
sector_rates.note: Tariffs eliminated per deal; listed on Aligned Partners exemption schedule.

tariff_tracker.section_122_surcharge: 10% global surcharge (Section 122, effective Feb 24 2026, expires Jul 24 2026)
tariff_tracker.last_change: Feb 24 2026 — White House imposed 10% Section 122 surcharge on all countries following Supreme Court Feb 20 2026 ruling striking down IEEPA tariffs (6-3)
tariff_tracker.next_trigger: Jul 24 2026 — Section 122 surcharge expiry (150-day statutory limit); Section 301 investigations ongoing (launched Mar 11 2026)

business_economy.headline: Seoul lowers fuel price caps and pledges to freeze utility rates in H2 2026
business_economy.body_text: South Korea lowered ceiling prices on fuel and vowed to freeze utility rates through the second half of 2026, citing easing global energy market volatility — with Brent crude falling 6.3% to $72.99 on June 26. The government also announced 350 billion won in agricultural product discount events to contain food inflation.

business_economy.headline: Hyundai Motor unveils revamped Avante sedan at Busan Mobility Show
business_economy.body_text: Hyundai Motor unveiled the all-new Avante sedan at the Busan Mobility Show, its flagship domestic automotive showcase event for 2026. The reveal underscores Hyundai's continued investment in core sedan lines alongside its EV expansion, which is tracked as part of the $26B US investment commitment.

business_economy.headline: South Korea's foreign currency deposits rise for second consecutive month in May
business_economy.body_text: Foreign currency deposits in South Korea increased for the second straight month in May, indicating sustained foreign capital presence despite equity market volatility. The trend provides a partial buffer against won depreciation pressure on days like June 26 when the KOSPI triggered circuit breakers.

business_economy.headline: Banks' household mortgage lending rates rise in May on higher market interest rates
business_economy.body_text: South Korean banks' household mortgage loan rates rebounded in May, tracking higher market interest rates even as the BOK held its benchmark at 2.50% after the June cut. Rising mortgage rates add a household financial stress dimension to the BOK's deliberations ahead of the July 10 rate decision.

business_economy.headline: Appellate court to rule next month on SK Group chief and ex-wife's asset division case
business_economy.body_text: A Seoul appellate court announced it will deliver its verdict next month on the asset division dispute between the SK Group chairman and his ex-wife. The case involves one of the largest conglomerate asset division proceedings in Korean legal history and has drawn sustained attention from institutional investors in SK affiliates.

northeast_asia.headline: President Lee meets Japanese chair of bilateral lawmakers' union, calls for closer ties
northeast_asia.body_text: President Lee Jae-myung met with the Japanese chair of the bilateral South Korea-Japan lawmakers' union and called for strengthening ties between the two countries. The meeting continues Lee's diplomatic outreach to Japan alongside the defense ministers' upcoming bilateral talks, sustaining the inter-parliamentary dimension of the relationship.

northeast_asia.headline: Expert: China seeks to 'normalize and substantialize' ties with North Korea while maintaining denuclearization stance
northeast_asia.body_text: A Korea expert assessed that Beijing is pursuing a dual-track policy of deepening practical relations with Pyongyang — including expanded tourism at Wonsan-Kalma — while officially maintaining denuclearization as a policy goal. The assessment follows the Xi-Kim summit and North Korea's preparations to expand the Wonsan-Kalma beach resort for Chinese tourists.

northeast_asia.headline: Ukraine and Japan discuss risks of Russia-DPRK cooperation and energy support
northeast_asia.body_text: Ukraine and Japan held consultations on the risks of Russia-DPRK military-technical cooperation and the issue of energy support for Ukraine, per Ukrainian national news reporting. The bilateral exchange reflects Japan's engagement in monitoring the NK-Russia weapons transfer axis — a direct concern for allied coalition cohesion.

gallup_spotlight.finding: Lee Jae-myung's approval rating fell to a record low of 51% since taking office, per Gallup Korea polling released June 26, with the ballot shortage scandal as the primary cited cause.

opeds_today.headline: Kim Jong Un vows military buildup as North Korea marks Korean War anniversary
opeds_today.central_argument: Kim Jong Un's Workers' Party plenum on the 76th Korean War anniversary formalized a new nationwide military buildup directive — including a coal mine modernization project — embedding the DPRK's 'overtake the world' military goal into party-state planning.
opeds_today.summary: NK Pro's state media review of the WPK plenum documents Kim Jong Un's vow to strengthen the military 'with a goal of overtaking the world' and announces a new national project to modernize coal mines — connecting civilian economic mobilization to military production capacity. The piece covers the anniversary context and the plenum's formal decisions. It provides the most detailed available open-source account of the plenum's substantive outputs.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: The plenum's formal directives — shipbuilding targets, nuclear posture language, coal mine modernization — are the primary reference document for assessing DPRK force development trajectories over the next five years.

kcna_delta.kim_activity: Kim Jong Un oversaw tests of weapons described by KCNA as designed to strike ROK power stations with tactical nuclear warheads, conducted on the 76th anniversary of the Korean War, following his June 25 commissioning of the Choe Hyon destroyer and Workers' Party plenum directives.

key_quotes.speaker: Kim Jong Un
key_quotes.quote: North Korea will offer full support and solidarity to the Russian army and people in carrying out the special military operation in Ukraine.
key_quotes.source_article: North Korean leader Kim oversees weapons tests to bolster firepower along border, KCNA says — Reuters

key_quotes.speaker: Kim Jong Un
key_quotes.quote: North Korea should build two warships a year in next five years.
key_quotes.source_article: North Korea's Kim claims progress on nuclear-armed navy as new warship is placed into service — The Washington Post

key_quotes.speaker: Kim Jong Un
key_quotes.quote: North Korea will exercise its position as a nuclear state.
key_quotes.source_article: North Korea's Kim calls for 'destructive' military posture as South vows to boost drones — The Washington Post

kcna_delta.bottom_line: KCNA reported Kim Jong Un overseeing tactical nuclear weapons tests explicitly targeting ROK power stations on the Korean War anniversary — the most operationally specific nuclear threat language since the June 25 plenum. Watch for KCNA publication of the full plenum resolution and any announcement of 10,000-ton missile cruiser construction timelines.
